title: Ray transfer (ABCD) matrix cascade
instruction: Implement free_space, thin_lens_matrix, and cascade in abcd.py. Matrices are 2x2 as nested lists [[A,B],[C,D]] acting on [y, u] where u is the ray angle. free_space(d) -> [[1, d],[0, 1]]. thin_lens_matrix(f) -> [[1, 0],[-1/f, 1]] for f!=0. cascade(matrices) multiplies matrices left-to-right as optical elements encountered by the ray (first element is leftmost / first encountered). Use M_total = M_n @ ... @ M_2 @ M_1. Raise ValueError for empty cascade or f==0.
difficulty_rationale: Matrix multiplication order is a common trap; cascade semantics must match optical convention.
